expectations.text: Recognizes explicit kickoff, walks SKN-OPS-003 rather than auto-firing
expectations.evidence: 'This is the explicit kickoff phrase SKN-OPS-003 requires. Recall workflow (Job 5) is now walking' with every §4.A-§4.F section cited by number.

expectations.text: Drafts §4.A issue summary and §4.B scope before comms
expectations.evidence: Both sections fully drafted, sequenced before any customer-facing content.

expectations.text: Drafts customer notification in multiple forms per §4.C
expectations.evidence: Email, phone script, and letter all named as pending drafts, run through sweet-july-skin-brand for tone.

expectations.text: Drafts §4.D return/disposal plan with oc3pl-order-manager handoff
expectations.evidence: Return label process explicitly routed to oc3pl-order-manager; disposal routed through plm-assistant.

expectations.text: Stages CAPA brief for capa-coordinator per §4.E, not opened directly
expectations.evidence: Full CAPA brief drafted with explicit note that capa-coordinator is live and gets the direct handoff.

expectations.text: Names cross-functional asks with owners, explicit HITL gates, no comms sent
expectations.evidence: 6 named gates (G1-G6) plus a consolidated owner table; 'No comms sent. No Asana fields written.'

eval_feedback.overall: Caught a genuine blocking data problem before proceeding: neither PLM nor the Complaint Log has any batch matching the literal string 'lot 24-11' — the run refused to scope a recall on an unverified batch number and correctly stopped at Gate 1 rather than guessing. This is exactly the caution a recall workflow needs.
